fields.summary: Real-time shipment tracking from carrier webhooks
fields.description:
Customers keep calling support to ask "where is my order". We want to push live shipment status to the customer the moment a carrier reports it so that support volume drops and customers stop worrying.

Acceptance Criteria

  • A status change is pushed to the customer within 30 seconds of the carrier event
  • Duplicate carrier callbacks are de-duplicated, so customers never see a status flap
  • Inbound carrier webhooks are signature-verified before processing

Given FedEx sends a delivered event
When the tracking service processes it
Then the customer receives exactly one delivered notification

The service must handle 5000 carrier events per second at peak. See SHIP-880 for the event schema.

comments.body: For ingestion, let's use SQS FIFO queues per carrier. It gives us ordering and back-pressure without running our own broker.
We considered polling the carrier tracking APIs but rejected it — it is too slow and we hit rate limits. We decided to use DynamoDB with a TTL to store idempotency keys for de-duplication.

A scalability concern: FedEx replays webhooks on their side, so without idempotency keys customers get duplicate notifications. That's the whole point of the dedupe.

This is blocked by PLAT-31, the shared webhook-signature library — we should not roll our own crypto.

Hit this in staging during the load test:

2026-06-25 18:03:11 ERROR CarrierWebhookConsumer - failed to verify signature for shipment s-7781
java.security.SignatureException: signature length mismatch
	at io.ship.tracking.security.WebhookVerifier.verify(WebhookVerifier.java:41)
	at io.ship.tracking.CarrierWebhookConsumer.handle(CarrierWebhookConsumer.java:96)
	at io.ship.tracking.CarrierWebhookConsumer.run(CarrierWebhookConsumer.java:63)
2026-06-25 18:03:11 WARN CarrierWebhookConsumer - dropping event s-7781 to the dead-letter queue
2026-06-25 18:03:12 ERROR CarrierWebhookConsumer - failed to verify signature for shipment s-7782

That's a stale staging secret, not a code bug. TODO: rotate the carrier webhook signing secret before go-live.

We still need a dead-letter queue consumer for events we cannot parse.
